response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with genuine geospatial exposure (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and real low-latency systems work in DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, which demonstrates aptitude for performance-critical Linux services. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his only professional C/C++ experience is a brief 7-month stint in 2014-2015; his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work. He also lacks direct experience with routing engines, map-tile pipelines, or navigation guidance systems, so the fit hinges on transferable systems skills rather than demonstrated domain expertise.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (founder of GISCollective, web GIS for Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers in DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and infrastructure experience
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience; no Rust — the role's primary technical requirement
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience at production scale
Recent years focused on full-stack web/TypeScript product work rather than distributed data pipelines
